paper_title: Moving beyond the age–depth model paradigm in deep-sea palaeoclimate archives: dual radiocarbon and stable isotope analysis on single foraminifera

methods.description: AMS analysis for direct 14C measurement of gaseous CO2 from CaCO3 samples, allowing for reduced sample mass and enabling single specimen analysis.

steps.description: AMS analysis was carried out at the Laboratory for Ion Beam Physics at ETH Zürich using a permanent magnet equipped 80 Mini Carbon Dating System (MICADAS) AMS with Helium stripping system coupled to an Ionplus carbonate handling system.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform AMS analysis using MICADAS for 14C measurement

steps.description: The MICADAS setup allows for direct 14C measurement, using a gas ion source, of gaseous CO2 liberated from CaCO3 samples by acidification with phosphoric acid.
steps.searchable_summary: Liberate CO2 from CaCO3 using phosphoric acid for 14C measurement

steps.description: Procedural single specimen foraminifera blank samples indicated an average blank value of 0.0115 F14C.
Benthic C. wuellerstorfi 14C ages were calibrated using MatCal 2.2, employing the Marine13 14C calibration curve and an appropriate marine reservoir age.
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate 14C ages using MatCal 2.2 and Marine13 calibration curve

methods.description: IRMS analysis on foraminifera test fractions to measure stable isotopes δ18O and δ13C with high precision.

steps.description: IRMS analysis on the smaller foraminifera test fractions was carried out using a Kiel IV carbonate device coupled to a Thermo MAT-253 dual inlet IRMS.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform IRMS analysis using Kiel IV and Thermo MAT-253

steps.description: Procedural standard samples indicated an external precision better than 0.10 ‰ and 0.05 ‰ for δ18O and δ13C, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Validate IRMS precision with standard samples

steps.description: Additional whole tests were analysed using a GasBench II preparation device coupled to a continuous flow Thermo Delta+ mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform additional IRMS analysis using GasBench II and Thermo Delta+

methods.description: Combined measurement of 14C and stable isotopes on single foraminifera to provide a temporal snapshot of ocean chemistry.

steps.description: Dual measurements of both 14C and stable isotopes on the same single specimen foraminifer in core T86-10P.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform dual 14C and stable isotope measurements on single foraminifera

steps.description: Successful dual 14C and stable isotope measurements reveal a C. wuellerstorfi benthic deglaciation signal.
steps.searchable_summary: Validate dual measurement results with benthic deglaciation signal
